message.content: {"schema_version":"stella.literature_hvs_candidates.v0.1","generated_at":"2026-06-13T11:24:54","paper":{"arxiv_id":"1901.04559","bibcode":"2019ApJ...872...58H","title":"Identifying Sagittarius Stream Stars By Their APOGEE Chemical Abundance Signatures","month":"2019-01","source_note_json":"notes/2019/2019-01/2019-01.json","links":{"abs":"https://arxiv.org/abs/1901.04559","pdf":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/1901.04559"}},"inputs":{"paper_dir":"literature/1901.04559","audit_path":"literature/1901.04559/audit.json","catalog_review_path":"literature/1901.04559/catalog_review.json","catalog_extraction_path":"literature/1901.04559/catalog_extraction.json","ecsv_paths":[]},"extraction":{"status":"candidates_found","extracted_at":"2026-06-13T11:24:54","extractor":"agent","summary":"One HVS candidate identified. At ~54 kpc Galactocentric distance with total velocity ~577^{+209}_{-129} km/s, the star is completely unbound from the Milky Way in MWPotential2014. Classified as early M-giant. Full Table 1 with APOGEE IDs and all 68 Sgr chemical candidates is not fully accessible from the provided sources; only the HVS candidate (membership class 'd') is individually identifiable from the paper text.","tooling":{"agent_runtime":"","model_id":"","prompt_version":"","request_parameters":{}}},"method_chain":[{"id":"step-01","depends_on":[],"step_type":"input_catalog","summary":"APOGEE DR14 survey. APOGEE-1 observed 146,000 stars; DR14 adds ~100,000 more in the Northern Hemisphere. High-resolution (R~22,500) near-infrared (1.51-1.70 μm) spectrograph on the Sloan 2.5m telescope. Stellar parameters and chemical abundances from ASPCAP pipeline.","inputs":["APOGEE DR14"],"outputs":["spectra","stellar_parameters","chemical_abundances","radial_velocities"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":159,"end_line":164,"context":"APOGEE survey description, DR14, ASPCAP pipeline"}]},{"id":"step-02","depends_on":["step-01"],"step_type":"cross_match","summary":"Cross-match APOGEE DR14 stars with Gaia DR2 proper motions using nearest-neighbor matching within 1 arcsec via TOPCAT. 62 of 68 chemical candidates have Gaia proper motion matches.","inputs":["APOGEE DR14 catalog","Gaia DR2"],"outputs":["matched_catalog_with_proper_motions"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":177,"end_line":179,"context":"Gaia DR2 cross-match with APOGEE using TOPCAT nearest-neighbor within 1 arcsec"}]},{"id":"step-03","depends_on":["step-02"],"step_type":"quality_filter","summary":"Select APOGEE stars with 3550<Teff<4200 K, S/N>70 per half resolution element, no ASPCAPBAD flag, and [Fe/H]>-1.2. These criteria match the Sgr core sample parameters.","inputs":["matched_catalog"],"outputs":["quality_filtered_sample"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":165,"end_line":173,"context":"Quality cuts: Teff 3550-4200 K, S/N>70, no ASPCAPBAD, [Fe/H]>-1.2"}]},{"id":"step-04","depends_on":["step-03"],"step_type":"distance_estimation","summary":"Spectro-photometric distances from the DR14 value-added catalog (NMSU_dist estimator from Hayden et al. 2014), accurate to ~20%. Adopted over Gaia parallax distances for homogeneity across the >5 kpc distance range.","inputs":["quality_filtered_sample"],"outputs":["distances"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":175,"end_line":175,"context":"Spectro-photometric distances from DR14 VAC, ~20% accuracy"}]},{"id":"step-05","depends_on":["step-01"],"step_type":"radial_velocity_measurement","summary":"Radial velocities measured by the APOGEE pipeline.","inputs":["APOGEE spectra"],"outputs":["radial_velocities"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":161,"end_line":164,"context":"APOGEE pipeline processes spectra to derive stellar parameters and RVs"}]},{"id":"step-06","depends_on":["step-03"],"step_type":"sample_selection","summary":"K-means clustering on 6D chemical abundance space [(C+N)/Fe, O/Fe, Mg/Fe, Al/Fe, Mn/Fe, Ni/Fe] to identify 68 Sgr chemical candidates. k=16 chosen so that 10% of Sgr core sample separates from the Sgr cluster.","inputs":["quality_filtered_sample","chemical_abundances"],"outputs":["68_Sgr_chemical_candidates"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":218,"end_line":242,"context":"K-means clustering on 6D chemical space with k=16"}]},{"id":"step-07","depends_on":[],"step_type":"solar_position_and_motion","summary":"Adopt R0=8.0 kpc, Vcirc=220 km/s at the Solar radius, and solar peculiar motion from Schonrich et al. (2010). Left-handed Galactic Cartesian coordinate system with X(U) positive toward Galactic center, Y(V) along rotation, Z(W) toward north Galactic pole.","inputs":[],"outputs":["solar_parameters"],"parameters":[{"name":"R0","raw_value":"8.0 kpc","value":"8.0","unit":"kpc","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":181,"context":"Solar radius of 8.0 kpc"}]},{"name":"v_circ_sun","raw_value":"220 km s^{-1}","value":"220","unit":"km/s","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":181,"context":"Circular velocity of 220 km/s at Solar radius"}]},{"name":"solar_motion_u","raw_value":"Solar motion as measured by Schonrich, Binney, and Dehnen (2010) - individual U component value not stated in paper","value":"not_reported","unit":"","description":"Individual U component value not stated; paper references Schonrich et al. (2010)","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":181,"context":"Solar motion from Schonrich et al. 2010, individual U value not stated"}]},{"name":"solar_motion_v","raw_value":"Solar motion as measured by Schonrich, Binney, and Dehnen (2010) - individual V component value not stated in paper","value":"not_reported","unit":"","description":"Individual V component value not stated; paper references Schonrich et al. (2010)","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":181,"context":"Solar motion from Schonrich et al. 2010, individual V value not stated"}]},{"name":"solar_motion_w","raw_value":"Solar motion as measured by Schonrich, Binney, and Dehnen (2010) - individual W component value not stated in paper","value":"not_reported","unit":"","description":"Individual W component value not stated; paper references Schonrich et al. (2010)","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":181,"context":"Solar motion from Schonrich et al. 2010, individual W value not stated"}]}],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":182,"context":"Solar parameters and coordinate system assumptions"}]},{"id":"step-08","depends_on":[],"step_type":"galactic_potential_model","summary":"MWPotential2014 from Bovy (2015) galpy: composite of spheroidal bulge, Miyamoto-Nagai disk, and NFW halo. Authors note halo mass may be too low but relative orbital properties are the focus.","inputs":[],"outputs":["MWPotential2014_potential"],"parameters":[{"name":"potential_name","raw_value":"MWPotential2014","value":"MWPotential2014","description":"Composite of spheroidal bulge, Miyamoto-Nagai disk, and NFW halo in galpy","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":182,"context":"MWPotential2014 potential description"}]}],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":182,"context":"Galactic potential model"}]},{"id":"step-09","depends_on":["step-04","step-05","step-02","step-07"],"step_type":"velocity_calculation","summary":"Calculate full 6D phase-space velocities (UVW) and total Galactocentric velocity using Gaia DR2 proper motions, APOGEE distances, and radial velocities, transformed to Galactocentric frame using solar parameters from step-07.","inputs":["distances","radial_velocities","proper_motions","solar_parameters"],"outputs":["UVW_velocities","total_galactocentric_velocity"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":182,"context":"Velocity calculation using proper motions, distances, RVs, and solar parameters"}]},{"id":"step-10","depends_on":["step-09","step-08"],"step_type":"orbit_integration","summary":"Integrate orbits forward and backward for 1.5 Gyr in MWPotential2014 using galpy. Initialize at each star's current position and velocity. Estimate orbital parameters.","inputs":["galactocentric_velocities","MWPotential2014_potential"],"outputs":["orbital_parameters"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":182,"context":"Orbit integration in MWPotential2014 for 1.5 Gyr forward and backward"}]},{"id":"step-11","depends_on":["step-10"],"step_type":"escape_or_bound_assessment","summary":"Determine bound/unbound status from integrated orbits. The HVS candidate's observed velocities suggest it is completely unbound from the Milky Way in MWPotential2014 (no measured apocenter, ~760 kpc at last timestep). Orbits for min/max space velocities within uncertainties are also unbound.","inputs":["orbital_parameters"],"outputs":["bound_status"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":296,"end_line":296,"context":"Star is unbound in MWPotential2014, no measured apocenter (~760 kpc at last timestep)"},{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":402,"end_line":402,"context":"Orbits for min/max space velocities within uncertainties are also unbound"}]},{"id":"step-12","depends_on":["step-11","step-09"],"step_type":"origin_assessment","summary":"The HVS candidate is an early M-giant with v_tot~577 km/s at ~54 kpc Galactocentric distance. Orbit does not trace back to Galactic center, LMC, or M31. Binary runaway unlikely for M-giant. Dynamical interaction considered but unlikely. Possible systematic error in Gaia proper motions noted.","inputs":["bound_status","orbital_parameters","stellar_type"],"outputs":["HVS_candidate_classification","origin_discussion"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":388,"end_line":403,"context":"HVS candidate origin discussion: early M-giant, orbit doesn't trace back to GC, LMC, or M31, systematic PM error possible"}]},{"id":"step-13","depends_on":["step-10"],"step_type":"other","summary":"Extract specific orbital parameters (eccentricity, peri- and apo-center distances, orbital energy, angular momentum) from the integrated orbit results for classification into orbital groups (Sgr stream, accreted halo, bulge, HVS, others).","inputs":["orbital_parameters"],"outputs":["eccentricity","r_peri","r_apo","orbital_energy","angular_momentum"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":182,"context":"Orbital parameter estimation: eccentricity, peri/apo-center, energy, angular momentum"},{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":290,"end_line":298,"context":"Classification of candidates into orbital groups based on eccentricity and r_apo"}]}],"candidates":[{"identifiers":{"record_id":"1901.04559:cand-001","paper_candidate_id":"HVS candidate","gaia_source_id":"","all":[{"value":"HVS candidate","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":291,"end_line":296,"context":"HVS (1 star) classification in orbital groups"}]},{"value":"membership class d","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":321,"end_line":321,"context":"Table 1 footnote: d = HVS"}]}]}}],"candidate_groups_considered":[{"group_id":"table-1-stream-candidates","description":"Full Sgr stream cluster from Table 1. Contains 68 stars: 35 Sgr stream (a), 20 accreted halo (b), 4 bulge (c), 1 HVS (d), 2 others (e). Only the HVS is possibly unbound.","decision":"excluded","reason":"Of 68 candidates, 35 are Sgr stream members on bound tidal debris orbits, 20 are accreted halo stars on bound eccentric orbits, 4 are bulge stars, and 2 are unclassified others. Only the 1 HVS candidate is unbound. Full Table 1 is available only from the online journal and not in the provided sources.","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":291,"end_line":298,"context":"Orbital groups: Sgr Stream (35), Accreted Halo (20), Bulge (4), HVS (1), Others (2)"},{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":304,"end_line":325,"context":"Table 1 header, partial entries, and footnote defining membership classes a-e"}]},{"group_id":"accreted-halo-group","description":"20 accreted halo stars with high eccentricities (e>0.75) and median r_apo=20.2 kpc, possibly from ancient major merger 8-11 Gyr ago. Sgr-like chemistry but bound orbits.","decision":"excluded","reason":"These stars are on bound orbits. The paper describes them as accreted halo stars, not as unbound/HVS candidates.","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":356,"end_line":361,"context":"Accreted halo stars: high eccentricities, median r_apo=20.2 kpc, bound orbits"}]},{"group_id":"sgr-stream-group","description":"35 Sgr stream members consistent with Law and Majewski (2010) model, stripped during last two pericenter passages.","decision":"excluded","reason":"These stars are Sgr tidal debris members on bound orbits within the Milky Way potential, not unbound/HVS candidates.","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":338,"end_line":344,"context":"35 Sgr stream stars with positions and kinematics consistent with LM10 model, bound tidal debris"}]}]}
